narrative: A small loose plant grows from the shaded bank, with reddish stems and little pink flowers held above divided leaves...
interesting_fact: When crushed, Herb Robert has a very strong smell, which is why one of its old common names is Stinking Bob.

questions.question: Which of these plants is Herb Robert?
Does Herb Robert have any other common names?
questions.remember: It gets this name from its strong smell when the leaves are crushed

questions.question: What is the botanical name of Herb Robert?
Where is Herb Robert typically found?
options: Woodland edges, shady banks, walls and other darker spots
Only in open marsh and reedbed
Only in intensively managed arable fields
questions.correct: Woodland edges, shady banks, walls and other darker spots

questions.question: Select all key identifying features of Herb Robert
options: Bright pink flowers with five petals
Hollow ridged stems with umbrella flower heads
correct: Bright pink flowers with five petals

questions.question: What is Herb Robert used for? Select all that apply
options: Traditionally used for loose bowels and digestive upset
Crushed and rubbed on skin as an insect repellent
Roots boiled as a staple food
correct: Traditionally used for loose bowels and digestive upset
Crushed and rubbed on skin as an insect repellent
questions.remember: The leaves have been used to make tea. Traditionally, infusions were also used for loose bowels and digestive upset, similar to bramble leaf

questions.question: What are the cautions with Herb Robert?
options: No known adverse effects — but always advise caution to customers when trying any wild plant for the first time
The flowers are poisonous unless cooked
The leaves must only be eaten after frost
Contact with the plant causes severe blistering in sunlight
questions.correct: No known adverse effects — but always advise caution to customers when trying any wild plant for the first time
questions.remember: Always advise caution to customers when consuming any wild plant, especially for the first time

questions.question: Is Herb Robert native to the UK?
options: Yes — native and widespread throughout Britain and Ireland
Yes — but only native in Scotland
questions.correct: Yes — native and widespread throughout Britain and Ireland

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Herb Robert?
questions.remember: Biennial means it usually completes its life cycle over two years — typically leafy growth in the first year, then flowering and seed in the second

questions.question: What plant family does Herb Robert belong to?
What does the name Robertianum refer to?
options: It is thought to refer to Robert, possibly an old personal or saint's name associated with the plant
questions.correct: It is thought to refer to Robert, possibly an old personal or saint's name associated with the plant
